ops.body: Premise check (2026-07-17): captain-hook's codex-lane nudges contain NO invocation/scratch mechanics to reword — commands.py's failure nudge just says "invoke /codex", and the routing judge prompts (review_routing_*/implementation_spawn_nudge.md) are pure model-routing, silent on -s/scratch/--await. Verified by repo grep: zero matches for reply_file / -s "$ROOT / --await in captain-hook. If this task's intent was "make guidance reflect codex-ask v2 auto-scratch", that landed in cc-skills instead: codex-ask 0.18.0 adds --mint-root (scratchpad-adopting fan-out roots + lane roster), and SKILL.md + the fleet claude-rules fragment now name it and forbid hand-minted scratch (.claude/scratch, ~/.claude). Any remaining work here would be NEW nudge authoring (e.g. a scratch-path guard), which the user explicitly descoped 2026-07-17 ("guidance only, no guard").
